ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Of Flavian Bishop of Antioch.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: About the same period, the following events took place at Antioch in Syria.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: After the death of Paulinus, the people who had been under his superintendence refused to submit to the authority of Flavian, but caused Evagrius to be ordained bishop of their own party.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: As he did not survive his ordination long, no other was constituted in his place, Flavian having brought this about: nevertheless those who disliked Flavian on account of his having violated his oath, held their assemblies apart.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Meanwhile Flavian ‘left no stone unturned,’ as the phrase is, to bring these also under his control; and this he soon after effected, when he appeased the anger of Theophilus, then bishop of Alexandria, by whose mediation he conciliated, Damasus bishop of Rome also.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: For both these had been greatly displeased with Flavian, as well for the perjury of which he had been guilty, as for the schism he had occasioned among the previously united people.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Theophilus therefore being pacified, sent Isidore a presbyter to Rome, and thus reconciled Damasus, who was still offended; representing to him the propriety of overlooking Flavian’s past misconduct, for the sake of producing concord among the people.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Communion being in this way restored to Flavian, the people of Antioch were in the course of a little while induced to acquiesce in the union secured.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Such was the conclusion of this affair at Antioch.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But the Arians of that city being ejected from the churches, were accustomed to hold their meetings in the suburbs.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Meanwhile Cyril bishop of Jerusalem having died about this time, was succeeded by John.
